title: Quiz: Logic Circuits
description: Test your understanding of combinational logic circuits

questions.question: In a half adder, what operation produces the Sum output?
questions.explanation: The Sum in a half adder is A XOR B. XOR outputs 1 when inputs differ, which matches single-bit addition without carry.

questions.question: What are the outputs of a full adder when A=1, B=1, Carry_in=1?
questions.explanation: 1+1+1=3 in decimal = 11 in binary. So Sum=1 (LSB) and Carry=1 (MSB).

questions.question: What is the circuit depth of an n-bit ripple-carry adder?
questions.explanation: In a ripple-carry adder, the carry must propagate through all n full adders sequentially, giving O(n) depth.

questions.question: A 4-to-1 multiplexer has 2 select lines.
questions.explanation: True! A 2^n-to-1 MUX needs n select lines. For 4 inputs, n=2 select lines can select from 2²=4 options.

questions.question: If a 2-to-1 MUX has d0=1, d1=0, and sel=1, what is the output?
questions.explanation: When sel=1, the MUX selects d1. Since d1=0, the output is 0.

questions.question: A demultiplexer with 2 select lines can route to how many outputs?
questions.explanation: With n select lines, a DEMUX can select from 2^n outputs. With 2 lines: 2²=4 outputs.

questions.question: Classical logic circuits are typically irreversible, while quantum circuits must be reversible.
questions.explanation: True! Classical gates like AND lose information (can't recover inputs from output). Quantum gates must be unitary (reversible) to preserve quantum information.

questions.question: How many full adders are needed for a 16-bit ripple-carry adder?
questions.explanation: An n-bit ripple-carry adder needs exactly n full adders, one for each bit position. So 16 bits needs 16 full adders.
